# CANDIDATE: CHART_STOP_ONLY_EXIT_BEARISH_REJECTION

**Filed:** 2026-06-01  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** exit_change  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

We hypothesize that using chart-stop as the sole exit mechanism (removing the premium stop) will reduce losses on losing J days while preserving wins on winning J days, because the chart stop is a more accurate representation of the trade invalidation (price closing back above the rejected level) than an arbitrary premium percentage. This edge exists because premature exits due to volatility-induced premium collapse (not actual trend reversal) are common in 0DTE options, and chart-stop aligns with the setup's core rejection logic.

## Mechanism

Entry logic remains unchanged (BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON trigger: level rejection + EMA ribbon flip + confluence). Exit logic: exit when SPY closes a 3-min candle above the rejected level + $0.50 buffer (chart stop) or at 15:50 ET time stop. The premium stop (-8%) is removed. All other management rules (TP1 at +30%, runner to breakeven after TP1, etc.) remain intact.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** $25K+ live account (for full qty), $1K paper account uses 3 contracts (approx 14% of headline P&L). Same as current strategy per risk-rules.md.
2. **Sample bias:** Testing on J-anchor days (6 days) + 05-26..29 window (4 days) = 10 days total. High overfit risk due to small sample and selection bias (J days are extreme outcomes; 05-26..29 window may not be representative).
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (requires walk-forward held-out window)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (requires check on top 3 J days with realistic simulator)
5. **Failure modes:** unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest (worst day, max drawdown, blow-up scenario)
6. **Concentration:** unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest (if top-5 days = X% of P&L, state X)

## Pre-merge gate

gym validators, walk-forward, real-fills

## Confidence

3 / 10 -- because we have no backtest results; the outcome is uncertain. Chart-stop may increase whipsaws in choppy markets or fail to capture runners if price doesn't close above ORH.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

Complements existing watchers and filters; does not conflict with any exit_change candidates in the leaderboard (none present). May complement parameter optimization candidates like #12 (V14E_PARAM_SWEEP_26K) by providing an alternative exit mechanism for the BEARISH_REJECTION setup. No overlap with watcher-only candidates (ranks 3-5, 8-11, 13-16, 18-21).
